text: A lemonade recipe uses 400 ml of water for every 100 ml of lemon juice. If Sam wants to make a smaller batch using only 50 ml of lemon juice, how much water does he need?
generatorSolution: The ratio of water to lemon juice is 400 ml : 100 ml, which simplifies to 4 : 1. This means for every 1 ml of lemon juice, Sam needs 4 ml of water. Sam is using 50 ml of lemon juice, so he needs 50 × 4 = 200 ml of water.
